name: Herb-Oatmeal Bread (Clay-pot)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the yeast, honey, and salt with 1 cup lukewarm (not hot) water. Set aside. If the water is too hot, it will kill the growing yeast.
2. Heat 1 cup water to boiling, then add oatmeal and sour cream. Simmer, covered, for 10 minutes. Remove from heat and cool to lukewarm.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine whole-wheat flour, seeds, herbs, cooled oatmeal mixture, and yeast mixture. Beat until well blended.
4. Add beaten egg and melted butter; beat several times again.
5. Add pine nuts, then gradually add 3 cups of all-purpose flour, beating after each addition until a fairly stiff dough forms.
6. On a floured surface, knead the remaining all-purpose flour into the dough about a dozen times, forming it into two large loaves.
7. Preheat oven to 480°F (250°C). Prepare two clay pots by soaking them in hot water for 15 minutes.
8. Place each loaf in an oiled pot and cover with aluminum foil, leaving only the top exposed. Allow dough to rise until nearly doubled in size (about 45 minutes).
9. Cover the pots and place them in the preheated oven. Bake for 40 minutes. Remove the lid for the last 5 minutes of baking if a golden brown crust is desired.
10. Remove from the oven, transfer loaves to plates, and serve. Pine nuts are optional; they can be omitted without significant impact on flavor.
(Note: This recipe produces high-quality bread with a crisp crust and moist interior. If the bread sticks to the pot, ensure it is lightly greased before baking.)
